matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a filaggrin epithelial-barrier thesis: the individual may carry a barrier-function signal relevant to atopic dermatitis, ichthyosis vulgaris, allergic sensitization, or atopic asthma context. Loss-of-function markers need zygosity and clinical-grade confirmation before escalation.

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: GT-confirmed rs61816761 (FLG R501X) dosage suggests this sample carries the FLG prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests a filaggrin epithelial-barrier thesis: the individual may carry a barrier-function signal relevant to atopic dermatitis, ichthyosis vulgaris, allergic sensitization, or atopic asthma context. Loss-of-function markers need zygosity and clinical-grade confirmation before escalation. This specific marker is curated as FLG loss-of-function barrier marker. rs61816761 (FLG R501X) is bundled as a FLG research marker for allergic disease, asthma, eczema, airway inflammation, or immune-regulatory cohort literature. Treat it as low-penetrance, cohort-aware association context rather than as a diagnostic or treatment-selection allele.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched rs61816761 (FLG R501X) at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the FLG prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: May tag regulatory, expression, coding, haplotype, or linked-locus variation around FLG.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a filaggrin epithelial-barrier thesis: the individual may carry a barrier-function signal relevant to atopic dermatitis, ichthyosis vulgaris, allergic sensitization, or atopic asthma context. Loss-of-function markers need zygosity and clinical-grade confirmation before escalation. An observed FLG variant places this sample in the curated FLG research context. The bundled FLG knowledge base is focused on asthma, allergy, atopic dermatitis, airway inflammation, epithelial-barrier biology, and immune-regulatory research. Seeded markers and locus models are intended as research context and should not be promoted to diagnostic, prognostic, or treatment-selection claims without external clinical curation. FLG loss-of-function alleles are higher-impact barrier-disruption markers than most common allergic-disease GWAS SNPs.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
research_focus: Allergic sensitization and atopic asthma context

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a FLG variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. The variant side of the thesis is: The variant observed in this sample suggests a filaggrin epithelial-barrier thesis: the individual may carry a barrier-function signal relevant to atopic dermatitis, ichthyosis vulgaris, allergic sensitization, or atopic asthma context. Loss-of-function markers need zygosity and clinical-grade confirmation before escalation. FLG methylation should be read as local regulatory context around an allergic-inflammation gene. Promoter-proximal or gene-body beta values may help frame accessibility or expression hypotheses, but they are not validated stand-alone biomarkers for asthma, eczema, allergy, immune activation, or treatment response. The bundled FLG knowledge base is focused on asthma, allergy, atopic dermatitis, airway inflammation, epithelial-barrier biology, and immune-regulatory research. Seeded markers and locus models are intended as research context and should not be promoted to diagnostic, prognostic, or treatment-selection claims without external clinical curation. FLG loss-of-function alleles are higher-impact barrier-disruption markers than most common allergic-disease GWAS SNPs.
